name: Paper Wrapped Chicken (Gee-bow Gai)
recipeIngredient: 45 Squares unwaxed butcher paper or aluminum foil, 6"x6"
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------MARINADE-------------------------
2 tablespoons Catsup
1 tablespoon Oyster sauce
1 1/2 tablespoons Hoisin sauce
1 tablespoon Thin soy sauce
1 tablespoon Rice wine
Dash of pepper
1 teaspoon Salt
1 teaspoon Sugar
2 teaspoons Cornstarch
---------------WRAPPING PAPER WRAPPED CHICK---------------
1. Skin and bone the chicken, then cut into pieces about 3 inches long by 2 inches wide.
2. Sprinkle chicken with each of the marinade ingredients listed above, mix well, and add green onions and ginger. Marinate for 2 hours.
3. See diagrams for wrapping instructions.
4. Heat oil to 325°F, then carefully place each package into the oil. Deep-fry 4 minutes on each side. Drain excess oil in a strainer before serving.
NOTE: Paper Wrapped Chicken may be deep-fried several hours in advance and reheated in an oven at 325°F for 10 minutes just before serving.
1. Brush center of paper with oil, and position one corner towards you. Place one piece of chicken and one sliver of green onion about 2 inches from that corner.
2. Fold the corner to cover the chicken.
3. Fold once more, approximately 1 1/4 inches, tucking the second edge under the meat (forming a triangle).
4. Make an envelope by folding the left and right corners toward the middle.
5. Tuck in the flap to close the envelope.
